answer: When a Cornell student withdraws or takes a leave, the leave is treated as a withdrawal for federal financial aid purposes, and Cornell must complete a Return of Title IV Funds (R2T4) calculation to determine the earned and unearned portions of Title IV aid as of the student’s last date of attendance. The student’s awards are then revised, and any unearned Title IV funds must be remitted to the U.S. Department of Education within 45 days of the date of institutional knowledge. Any remaining Title IV credit balance funds must be paid to the student or the student’s parents/family within 14 days of the R2T4 calculation. Cornell also reports withdrawals and leaves to NSLDS within 60 days of institutional knowledge.

answer: A model College Academic Integrity Hearing Board consists of a chairperson who is a faculty member, three faculty members, three students, and a nonvoting record-keeper. The chairperson is appointed by the dean for a two-year term; the faculty members are elected for three-year terms (except at the School of Continuing Education and Summer Sessions, where they are appointed by the dean); and the students are elected by the student body or appointed by the dean for at least one year, preferably two-year terms. The record-keeper is nonvoting. At hearings, at least two-thirds of the voting Board members must be present, including two students and two faculty members.

answer: The provided evidence does not say whether a student can keep Cornell email access while on a Health Leave of Absence (HLOA). It only states that when a student’s enrollment status changes, automated notification alerts departments including financial aid, housing, the student health plan, international services, athletics, instructors, and the faculty advisor, and that the change may affect financial aid, billing, student health benefits, and visa status. It does not mention Cornell email access.
